code_runs.code:
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from utils.momentum import rank_by_momentum, calculate_momentum

# Download data
tsla = yf.download("TSLA", start="2015-01-01", end="2025-01-01", interval="1mo", auto_adjust=True, progress=False)
tsla_daily = yf.download("TSLA", start="2015-01-01", end="2025-01-01", interval="1d", auto_adjust=True, progress=False)

# Flatten columns if MultiIndex
if isinstance(tsla.columns, pd.MultiIndex):
    tsla.columns = tsla.columns.get_level_values(0)
if isinstance(tsla_daily.columns, pd.MultiIndex):
    tsla_daily.columns = tsla_daily.columns.get_level_values(0)

prices_monthly = tsla["Close"].dropna().tolist()
prices_daily   = tsla_daily["Close"].dropna().tolist()

print(f"Monthly prices: {len(prices_monthly)}, Daily prices: {len(prices_daily)}")

# ── Momentum across lookback periods ──────────────────────────────────────────
momentum_results = {}
for period in [1, 3, 6, 12, 24]:
    mom = calculate_momentum(prices_monthly, period=period)
    momentum_results[period] = mom
    print(f"  {period:2d}-month momentum: {mom:+.1f}%")

code_runs.code:
# ── ANALYSIS 2: Autocorrelation of returns ────────────────────────────────────
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.stats.stattools import durbin_watson

monthly_close = tsla["Close"].dropna()
monthly_ret   = monthly_close.pct_change().dropna()
daily_close   = tsla_daily["Close"].dropna()
daily_ret     = daily_close.pct_change().dropna()

# Autocorrelations at lags 1–12 (monthly) and 1–20 (daily)
monthly_acf = [monthly_ret.autocorr(lag=i) for i in range(1, 13)]
daily_acf   = [daily_ret.autocorr(lag=i)   for i in range(1, 21)]

print("=== Monthly Return Autocorrelations (lags 1–12) ===")
for lag, acf in enumerate(monthly_acf, 1):
    bar = "█" * int(abs(acf) * 40)
    sign = "+" if acf >= 0 else "-"
    print(f"  Lag {lag:2d}: {acf:+.3f}  {sign}{bar}")

print("\n=== Daily Return Autocorrelations (lags 1–20) ===")
for lag, acf in enumerate(daily_acf, 1):
    bar = "█" * int(abs(acf) * 40)
    sign = "+" if acf >= 0 else "-"
    print(f"  Lag {lag:2d}: {acf:+.3f}  {sign}{bar}")

# Durbin-Watson on monthly returns (near 2 = no autocorrelation)
dw = durbin_watson(monthly_ret.values)
print(f"\nDurbin-Watson (monthly): {dw:.3f}  (2=no autocorr, <2=pos, >2=neg)")

code_runs.code:
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
from matplotlib.patches import FancyBboxPatch
from statsmodels.stats.stattools import durbin_watson
from scipy import stats
from utils.momentum import calculate_momentum

# ── Download data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
tsla_m = yf.download("TSLA", start="2015-01-01", end="2025-01-01", interval="1mo",
                     auto_adjust=True, progress=False)
tsla_d = yf.download("TSLA", start="2015-01-01", end="2025-01-01", interval="1d",
                     auto_adjust=True, progress=False)

for df in [tsla_m, tsla_d]:
    if isinstance(df.columns, pd.MultiIndex):
        df.columns = df.columns.get_level_values(0)

close_m = tsla_m["Close"].dropna()
close_d = tsla_d["Close"].dropna()
ret_m   = close_m.pct_change().dropna()
ret_d   = close_d.pct_change().dropna()

prices_monthly = close_m.tolist()

# ── Momentum across lookback periods ─────────────────────────────────────────
periods = [1, 3, 6, 12, 24]
mom_vals = [calculate_momentum(prices_monthly, p) for p in periods]

# ── Autocorrelation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
monthly_acf = [ret_m.autocorr(lag=i) for i in range(1, 13)]
daily_acf   = [ret_d.autocorr(lag=i) for i in range(1, 21)]
dw          = durbin_watson(ret_m.values)

# ── Rolling 12-month momentum ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
rolling_mom = []
rolling_dates = []
for i in range(12, len(close_m)):
    window = close_m.iloc[i-12:i+1].tolist()
    rolling_mom.append(calculate_momentum(window, 12))
    rolling_dates.append(close_m.index[i])
rolling_mom   = pd.Series(rolling_mom,   index=rolling_dates)

# ── Autocorrelation significance bounds (95%) ─────────────────────────────────
n_m = len(ret_m)
n_d = len(ret_d)
sig_m = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_m)
sig_d = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_d)

# ── Momentum persistence: does a good month predict the next? ─────────────────
# Quintile analysis of monthly returns
ret_m_df = ret_m.to_frame("ret")
ret_m_df["next_ret"] = ret_m_df["ret"].shift(-1)
ret_m_df["quintile"] = pd.qcut(ret_m_df["ret"], 5, labels=["Q1\n(Worst)","Q2","Q3","Q4","Q5\n(Best)"])
quintile_means = ret_m_df.groupby("quintile", observed=True)["next_ret"].mean() * 100

# ── Hurst Exponent (R/S analysis) ────────────────────────────────────────────
def hurst_exponent(ts, min_lag=2, max_lag=100):
    """Estimate Hurst exponent via R/S analysis."""
    ts = np.log(ts).diff().dropna().values
    lags = range(min_lag, max_lag)
    rs_vals = []
    for lag in lags:
        chunks = [ts[i:i+lag] for i in range(0, len(ts)-lag, lag)]
        rs_chunk = []
        for chunk in chunks:
            if len(chunk) < 2:
                continue
            mean_c  = np.mean(chunk)
            devs    = np.cumsum(chunk - mean_c)
            r       = np.max(devs) - np.min(devs)
            s       = np.std(chunk, ddof=1)
            if s > 0:
                rs_chunk.append(r / s)
        if rs_chunk:
            rs_vals.append(np.mean(rs_chunk))
    if not rs_vals:
        return np.nan
    slope, intercept, *_ = stats.linregress(np.log(list(lags)[:len(rs_vals)]),
                                             np.log(rs_vals))
    return slope

hurst_m = hurst_exponent(close_m)
hurst_d = hurst_exponent(close_d)

print(f"Hurst Exponent (monthly): {hurst_m:.3f}")
print(f"Hurst Exponent (daily):   {hurst_d:.3f}")
print(f"Durbin-Watson (monthly):  {dw:.3f}")
print(f"\nQuintile next-month returns:")
print(quintile_means)
print(f"\nMonthly autocorrelations (lag 1–12): {[round(x,3) for x in monthly_acf]}")
print(f"Daily   autocorrelations (lag 1–5):  {[round(x,3) for x in daily_acf[:5]]}")
